steps.title: Block the basket body (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a wide, slightly tapered shape — like a bucket — narrower at the base than at the rim. Add a shallow ellipse across the top for the opening.

steps.title: Plan the handle (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a tall curving arc rising from one side of the rim, over the basket, and back down to the other side. Let it sit a comfortable distance above the rim — handles are taller than you'd think.

steps.title: Place the produce (pencil)
steps.description: Inside and rising over the rim, sketch three or four overlapping rounded shapes — a couple of apples or lemons, maybe a leafy bunch poking out. Let them overlap each other rather than sitting in a neat row.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Trace the basket body, the handle arc, and the tops of the produce that rise above the rim. Stop the back rim line where the produce covers it — don't draw through.

steps.title: Ink the weave texture (ink)
steps.description: On the basket body, add two or three horizontal bands of short alternating diagonal marks — a basket-weave hint. Let the marks suggest the weave; don't fill every square inch.

steps.title: Ink the produce (ink)
steps.description: Trace each piece of produce as a confident curve. Add a stem or a leaf to one or two pieces. Where one fruit sits in front of another, stop the back outline cleanly at the crossing.

steps.title: Add handle wrap (ink)
steps.description: Along the handle arc, add a few short cross-marks to suggest where the handle is wrapped or woven. A handful is plenty.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove any pencil guide lines that distract from the ink.
